$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:scene_ethics block (Nonfiction Pre-Draft Pathway, Increment 4 — the scene-ethics / consent plan). One block per identifiable real person depicted in narrative nonfiction / memoir. This is the writer's pre-draft ETHICAL plan — consent status, how the depiction will be handled, and the fairness reasoning — kept DISTINCT from the Legal Risk Register (apodictic.legal_risk.v1), which owns LEGAL exposure; the two coexist and cross-reference via the optional `legal_ref` (an LR-NN id). `consent_status`: obtained | sought-pending | not-sought | not-applicable (public figure acting publicly / deceased / fully fictionalized). `handling`: as-is | anonymize | composite | seek-consent | omit. Ids use the EP-NN namespace (Ethics Plan), distinct from the State Card's SE-NN. The Firewall holds: the writer makes the ethical decisions; the engine surfaces unresolved depictions to plan, it does not adjudicate ethics or write the scenes, and it is not legal advice. Validated by scripts/scene_ethics.py. Closed-key (additionalProperties:false) enforced by the engine (Harness Contracts v2): all optional fields (fairness_check, legal_ref) are declared, so a misspelled field now fails. Declared closed in schemas/_coverage.json. See docs/nonfiction-pre-draft.md.
title: APODICTIC scene-ethics item (apodictic.scene_ethics.v1)
